text.title: writing-implementation-plans Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for writing-implementation-plans as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Task with acceptance criteria that say "works correctly" or "functions as expected".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Vague AC cannot be verified by a test or a quality gate check. Every AC must specify a concrete observable outcome: a status code, a file that exists, a database row, a rendered element.

failure-mode.description: Agent declares all tasks as Wave 1 (no dependencies) to maximize apparent parallelism.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Missing dependencies cause merge conflicts and broken builds when parallel agents overwrite each other's files. Require dependency evidence from design.md for every "no dependencies" claim.

failure-mode.description: Agent declares all tasks as depending on the previous one, producing a purely sequential plan.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs default to sequential thinking. A purely sequential plan with more than 3 tasks likely contains false dependencies. Review each FS edge: does the downstream task actually need the upstream file to exist, or is it just logically related?

failure-mode.description: Task token estimate over 100k with no split.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Declare the task as "Split Required" and decompose into subtasks before proceeding. A 150k task is not one complex task — it is two or three tasks with a shared header.

failure-mode.description: Task references another task in "Depends on" that does not exist in the plan.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Add the missing task or fix the reference. Missing dependency tasks are the second most common cause of execution failures after token overruns.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Restructure the dependency chain. Circular deps indicate a design problem: one of the two tasks needs to be split, or one dependency is false (logically related but not a real FS constraint).

failure-mode.description: File structure section listing non-existent files or files that cannot be split as declared.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Verify every listed file against the actual codebase. Executor agents will either create orphaned files or fail when files they need do not exist.
